name: Christmas Ginger Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour, baking soda, ginger, cloves, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt in a bowl.
2. In another bowl, beat together shortening, molasses, brown sugar, water, egg, and vanilla until well blended.
3. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and mix until just combined. The dough will be soft.
4. Divide the dough into four parts. Roll each part into a 1-inch thick round on a lightly floured surface.
5. Wrap the rounds in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 3 hours or up to 1 week.
6. When ready to bake, roll out each round to 1/8-inch thick, cut with gingerbread cutters, and place on baking sheets.
7. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10-12 minutes.
8. Allow cookies to cool slightly on the baking sheet, then transfer to wire racks to cool completely.
9. For Royal Icing: In a large bowl, mix confectioners' sugar, cream of tartar, and egg whites until stiff peaks form. Add food coloring as desired. Use the icing to decorate cooled cookies.
This recipe makes a generous batch of ginger cookies that are easy to handle and can be made ahead of time. The dough can be refrigerated for up to one week before baking.
